headline: PKCS#12 public-key integrity is produced and verified (RFC 7292 sec. 4).
summary: pki.pkcs12 gains the second RFC 7292 integrity mode. A PKCS#12 store can be protected by a keypair signature instead of a password MAC: pki.pkcs12.build with opts.integrity.mode 'public-key' wraps the AuthenticatedSafe in a CMS SignedData -- a signature from any pki.cms.sign signer (RSA, ECDSA, EdDSA, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, or composite), with no MacData -- and pki.pkcs12.open verifies that signature FIRST, as the integrity gate, before decrypting or returning any bag. The signer is surfaced as a per-signer verdict but is never trust-chained: a valid signature authenticates the store's integrity, not the signer's identity, and anchoring the signer certificate to a trust root remains the caller's pki.path.validate step. Privacy is independent of integrity, so a public-key-integrity store's bags are still PBES2-encrypted under the password; a wrong bag password there is the uniform decrypt-failed verdict. A tampered store, an invalid signature, or a signer-less SignedData fails closed with pkcs12/signature-invalid, and open returns nothing. The build composes pki.cms.sign and open composes pki.cms.verify over the exact AuthenticatedSafe bytes the signature covers -- no re-serialization -- and the store round-trips through our own open and an independent OpenSSL cms verification of the authSafe.

Added: pki.pkcs12.build produces a public-key-integrity PKCS#12 store (RFC 7292 sec. 4). With opts.integrity { mode: 'public-key', signer: { cert, key, digestAlgorithm?, pss? } | signers: [ ... ], sid?, signingTime?, certificates? } it wraps the AuthenticatedSafe in a CMS SignedData whose id-data eContent is the byte-exact AuthenticatedSafe, signed by any pki.cms.sign signer algorithm (RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 / RSASSA-PSS, ECDSA, EdDSA, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, composite ML-DSA), with no MacData. Combining opts.mac with public-key integrity, or building with no signer, is a config-time pkcs12/bad-integrity-mode or pkcs12/bad-input. Privacy is unchanged: opts.password still PBES2-encrypts the bags.

pki.pkcs12.open verifies a public-key-integrity store. It runs pki.cms.verify over the store's CMS SignedData authSafe FIRST -- the integrity gate, exactly as the MAC is for password mode -- and returns nothing on a failure (pkcs12/signature-invalid), before decrypting any bag. The result bundle gains signers, the per-signer verdict [{ ok, sid, cert }] (null in password / MAC-less mode); the signer certificate is surfaced but never chained to a trust anchor -- anchoring it is the caller's pki.path.validate step. opts.signerCerts supplies the signer certificate for a store built with certificates: false. The bags then decrypt under the caller password exactly as in password mode (privacy is independent of integrity); a wrong bag password is the uniform pkcs12/decrypt-failed.
